kept.text:
$IREN

미란티스 인수정리

IREN은 지금까지 전력 모트 + GPU 모트
두 개의 해자를 가진 회사였음
근데 이번 인수로 세 번째 해자가 생길 수 있다고 봄

미란티스가 뭐하는 회사냐면

-GPU 수천 개를 동시에 관리·배분해주는 소프트웨어 회사
-엔터프라이즈 고객 1,500개 이상
-Adobe, PayPal, DocuSign 같은 대기업이 이미 쓰고있음
-엔비디아가 AI 클라우드 파트너로 공식 인증 회사 중 하나

IREN은 지금까지 GPU 하드웨어만 빌려줬음
고객이 그걸 어떻게 쓸지 직접 세팅해야 했고,
그러니까 딴 데로 옮기는 것도 어렵지 않았다.

근데 미란티스를 얹으면 달라짐

GPU 위에 "어떻게 쓸지"를 책임지는 소프트웨어가 생기고
고객이 그 위에서 일을 시작하면
환경을 통째로 옮겨야 하니까 이탈 비용이 올라간다.

AWS가 강한 이유가 서버 자체가 아니라
그 위에 쌓인 수백 개의 서비스 때문인 것처럼.

수직통합의 완성도는
전력 → 데이터센터 → GPU → 운영 SW → 엔터프라이즈 지원 

경쟁사 중 이 구조를 가진 네오클라우드는 없다
해자는 선언이 아니라 ARR로 증명된다

내일 어닝콜에서 확인하시죠

kept.text:
$IREN acquiring Mirantis for $625M is not a “software add-on.”

It’s a move into sovereign AI infrastructure.

Mirantis serves 1,500+ enterprise customers and powers Kubernetes, OpenStack, Docker, hybrid cloud, and AI orchestration infrastructure for companies like Adobe, PayPal, DocuSign, and major banks, telecoms, pharma, and defense organizations. (IREN)

This matters because the future of AI won’t belong only to hyperscalers.

Governments, banks, telecoms, healthcare, and defense firms increasingly want:
✅ sovereign compute
✅ private AI infrastructure
✅ full control over their software stack
✅ on-prem + hybrid AI deployments

Mirantis gives IREN the orchestration layer on top of its GPU/data center infrastructure.

Even more important:
✅ $100M+ revenue
✅ cashflow positive
✅ contributor to Docker, Kubernetes & OpenStack
✅ NVIDIA AI Cloud Ready partner
✅ 100% stock deal = long-term alignment 

This pushes IREN beyond “GPU capacity provider.”

They’re building a vertically integrated AI cloud platform.

kept.text:
$PYPL Lo que piensa uno de los mejores inversores del mundo, Michael Burry, parece que iba bastante en la dirección que yo veía… aunque también pienso que actualmente tenemos en el mercado oportunidades más sencillas y claras para obtener este potencial.

Michael Burry sobre PayPal:

• Cree que PayPal podría convertirse desde estos niveles en una inversión compuesta al 16-17% anual a largo plazo.

• Destaca que la compañía está recomprando acciones de forma muy agresiva:

6.000 millones de dólares en buybacks sobre una capitalización de unos 41.000 millones y prácticamente sin deuda neta. Esa era una de las patas importantes de mi tesis cuando la compré.

• Considera que el mercado no está valorando correctamente el efecto de esas recompras ni el posible turnaround con el nuevo CEO.

• Piensa que la IA permitirá reducir costes y plantilla de forma importante en los próximos años, y que el nuevo CEO se centrará en agilizar la empresa.

• También deja caer que PayPal podría acabar siendo objetivo de private equity o de una adquisición estratégica… una OPA, algo que yo ya os comenté en febrero, cuando cotizaba un 25% más barata.

Burry cree que el mercado podría estar infravalorando su capacidad de recuperación… aunque la gran pregunta para mí es si ahora mismo tenemos oportunidades más claras y sencillas en el mercado.

profile_bio.description: Founder InvirtiendoME 📝 https://t.co/aT5v9vSyxf PRO Investor @etoro Nominado mejor inversor 2023,2024 ,2025 -No es consejo financiero

kept.text:
Is AI replacing human's workforce?

The crypto and payment world is seeing a wave of massive layoffs, and many CEOs are pointing the finger at "AI transition" as the reason.

Since Block cut its staff in half earlier this year, big names like Coinbase, PayPal, and Gemini have all followed suit, claiming they need to become "lean" and "AI-native."

PayPal, for instance, plans to cut 20% of its team to save $1.5 billion, while 0G Labs recently let go of a quarter of its workers.

However, many experts aren't buying the AI excuse. They call it "AI washing", using tech buzzwords to hide basic business struggles.

The reality is that #Bitcoin is down significantly from its recent peak, trading is slow, and competition is fierce.

Companies like Block simply hired too many people during the boom years, and now they are forced to trim the fat.

Investors are reacting with mixed feelings:
 
Block’s stock jumped 38% after their announcement.
 
PayPal saw a 12% dip.

Coinbase shares also slid about 4%.

Whether these cuts are truly about high-tech efficiency or just a way to survive a tough market remains to be seen.

Shared via CoinEx Creator Program. @CoinExCreators
#CoinEx #CoinExCreator
